name: Operation Wolf Returns: First Mission VR

reviews.review_description: Hello this game Todd crisis kind of shooter cartoonish time passing game it's cool something different than what I have in my library keep up the good work looking forward to updates more levels more weapons

I got this on sale for £4.99. I hate to diss games but it’s not even worth that. It’s not bad, it’s just not good or fun compared to so many other options available today. If it was released for the original Quest for the same price, great, it would be an option back then, but then there wasn’t much choice. There is now and this has a ridiculous rrp that is in no way justified. 

I won’t repeat what many others have said before, but I got to the first boss level and stopped and assumed the game would save. It didn’t. Why!? I had to start again from the start and got there again but just could not be bothered. It’s just not fun. The wave shooter option is not either and really should not be on 360 degrees or at least have an option for 180 or less. Check out dead second. That is £8.99 as I write this, it offers so much more in every way. 

O hate to say, but it really seems the devs didn’t seem to care when making this game and having never played the original I checked a video of it and it looked so much more fun than this effort in every way. A shame, but will be requesting a refund after not even 40 minutes of play. Giving a generous 2 stars. If you played the original then it maybe enjoyable for nostalgia reasons, if not I’d steer clear.

Really well made homage to the classic arcade game. Fun, visually appealing graphics, easy to pick up & play. My inner child is grinning every time I play. Hats off to the devs - this is what VR is all about

I'm playing in seated mode and having to fight my chair enemies spawn behind me??
Yeah makes sense not
It's basic graphics arcade shooter if that's what you want. Defo refunding

A fun homage to an 80s arcade classic. Shooting for fun, not overly complicated and tongue in cheek humour. Lots of playability, and all-round smiles if you like retro 80s gameplay.

You’ll be disappointed if you’re expecting a modern shooter like Fortnight. But if you enjoyed the arcade original you will like this.
Back to child days.  Love

reviews.review_title: Not bad but not so good
reviews.review_description:
Cons:
- attacks from behind you (bad concept for a rail shooter ???)
- errors in map design (gaps)
- missing visual sign of finished campaigns
- script bugs (spawning to early)
- bad control for the helicopter gun
- each time you start the game, you have to press the skip button
- low resolution baked lights
- annoying when you have low life and the vision is limited
- very bad sound design (you can't locate the source of sound)

Pros:
+ nice, stylistics graphics
+ varied maps
+ unique boss fights
+ helicopter mission
+ waves mode
+ different paths
+ bullets are clearly visible
+ you can press the continue button as many times as you want (but respawns count are not saved) 

72/100
I absolutely love this game. It was entirely too short though. I Want more! more More More! Give us more of these kind of shooters! Aliens, machines, zombies, terrorists. The works! Give More!!

reviews.review_title: Not a sit down game as advertised.
reviews.review_description: Maybe if you are sitting in a spinning chair that doesn't have arm rests. But not a sit down game

It's a rail shooter like it always was. It's fairly casual and simple. People seem to be expecting a massive campaign that's super serious. If it was I would refund it because then they would just jumping on the shooter bandwagon. My biggest gripe is the graphics and resolution. The graphics remind me of Virtua fighter and it looks really jaggy. Other than that it's simple casual fun. It's not Call of duty. 🤷‍♂️

reviews.review_title: No Response On Any Review From Developer
reviews.review_description: I am concerned that none of the reviews, especially critical ones have not had any responses. That typically is a red flag to not expect many if any updates addressing issues. I hope I'm proven wrong.
This game is fun I've played a little haven't gotten to the end yet but so far so good brings back the nintendo days amd the enemy ways one is great survival game

I remember playing this game in the arcades when I was young. Never could get very far, and always ran out of quarters.  But now with the VR version I was able to finish the entire game! 

The story portion of the game was fun. The horde mode was too easy and I was able to run all 99 levels in one try. Needs to be harder. Too many tanks, helicopters and tanks do not shoot at all. 
Very repetitive.

This is the perfect VR game.

Pros: 
- Based off of a classic arcade smash hit, 1987’s “Operation Wolf” by Taito
- Very comfortable to play standing or sitting 
- Action packed enough to excite, yet casual enough not to frustrate 
- Nothing complicated about shooting nor reloading, yet varied enough to keep from getting bored
- Appealing for all ages

Requested Additions (not cons):
- Achievements. Would be great to have goals to work towards. The console ports have achievements.
- The original 1987 arcade game. It should be an unlockable bonus for beating the game. Seeing the original arcade cabinet on the menu is such a tease.
- Interactive menu. Speaking of teases, there’s so many targets and destructible-looking objects that are just static parts of the set. Let people new to VR shoot EVERYTHING.
- More modes. The 99 Waves were fun, but I got an S-Rank on my first attempt and cleared all 99 waves in 35 minutes. Gotta add some more stuff. Maybe unlockable weapons or silly modifiers for the solo campaign. Maybe “Operation Thunderbolt” or “Operation Wolf 3”.

It's a rail-shooter that takes you through 6 levels which are about 5 to 10 minutes each. It seems unnecessarily short. It lacks a sense of danger since it takes a lot of hits to actually lose. Shooting-game fans will likely be disappointed with it but ok for casual gamers. On the positive side, it feels like a good target practice simulator and it was entertaining while it lasted. I played Operation Wolf on Nintendo back in the early-90s. That nostalgic feeling drew me to this game.

Those 80's vibes ❤️.
So much memories playing this on my sega master system as a child.
I wish there was a coop mode and an enhanced version for the quest 3.
Tutorial was glitched for me.
When i needbto check te watch to see my health. It keeps rumbling and it does nothing.
So i just started mission 1.
But the left controller never stopped rumbling.
I had to quit and restart the game.
I wish there was more rail shooter in vr.
Imagine time crisis 1 and 2 in vr....
What a blast it would be.
I know i know. These are namco titles and not taito 😅

I remember playing the original in the arcade so this game brought some of those memories. 

It is an enjoyable experience but like others have said, it's way too short for the price.

I also encountered a bug where the game would not let me switch weapons. No matter how many times I pushed the direction of the gun I wanted, it wouldn't switch. To fix it, I had to switch to a gun I didn't want and then to the one I actually wanted. This led to some cheap deaths, which was a little frustrating. Hopefully, they will fix this with an update.

Remember playing this on my C64 with a light gun on the old crt tv,what a classic .
Was better on the C64,lol.

Will update more later.

Way too short,don't bother for the price .

Save your hard earned cash for the new upcoming AAA titles.

It's a great game but it was too short of a Game to play for the amount it is!
 I hope the Developers put  more levels in it!  

I also hope they come out with the Game Called 
         " Commando "
Amazing, but short
